description_long: This chapel is a small, stone-built religious structure with a rectangular nave and a prominent, slender steeple rising from one end. The exterior walls are constructed from light-colored stone blocks, featuring tall, narrow arched windows with intricate tracery, reminiscent of Gothic architecture. The roof is pitched and covered with gray tiles, while the steeple is capped with a pointed spire and includes small lancet windows. The main entrance is a pointed arch door, painted a deep red, set within a stone frame at the base of the steeple. Decorative buttresses and window surrounds add to the vertical emphasis and ornate detailing. The chapel sits on a flat, brown base that simulates a ground surface, suggesting placement in an urban or park setting.
description: A small stone chapel with a tall steeple, arched windows, and a red pointed entrance door, featuring Gothic architectural details and a pitched gray roof.
description_view_0: Side view showing two large arched windows with tracery and a small side window, plus the steeple rising from the rear.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the pointed red entrance door at the base of the steeple and symmetrical stonework.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with two large arched windows and the steeple visible at the far end.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a single arched window and the back of the pitched roof, with the steeple in the background.
attribute_stained_glass_windows: The chapel features tall, arched windows with tracery that suggest the presence of stained glass, but no specific imagery or color themes are visible.
attribute_steeple_or_bell_tower: The chapel has a tall, slender steeple located at one end, featuring a pointed spire and small lancet windows, characteristic of Gothic design.
attribute_entrance_door_design: The main entrance door is pointed-arched, painted deep red, and set within a stone frame, with a simple yet elegant design that complements the Gothic style. No elaborate carvings or additional decorative elements are visible.
